# UX Requirements Quality Checklist: Website Improvements

**Purpose**: Validate that UX requirements (credibility, scannability, About as hub, theme descriptors, project discoverability) are complete, unambiguous, and testable. Tests the quality of what is specified, not implementation behavior.  
**Created**: 2026-02-28  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Is at least one type of concrete credibility signal (e.g. previous role, thing built, current initiative by name) explicitly required in the hero, or is any single signal acceptable? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, User Story 1]
- [ ] CHK002 Are "major homepage sections" enumerated so that each must have a heading and brief summary per FR-002? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the full set of content required on the About page (headshot, companies/roles/timelines, social links) explicitly listed and sufficient for User Story 1? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK004 Is the homepage required to link to About with specific copy (e.g. "More about me") or is any link acceptable? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK005 Is the source or format for theme descriptors (e.g. config map, frontmatter) specified so FR-004 is implementable? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK006 When the site has only one project, are requirements specified (e.g. filter/group UI optional or hidden)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "brief summary or subheading" per section defined (e.g. one line, word limit) or left to interpretation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "15-second scan" defined (e.g. time-bound test, or qualitative "scannable without scrolling past fold") so SC-001 is verifiable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, SC-001]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "short descriptor" for themes defined (e.g. character limit, single phrase) so the writing index stays consistent? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "filter or group by at least one dimension" explicitly tied to existing frontmatter (type, status) or could new dimensions be added? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK011 When a filter is applied, is "selection is clear" defined (e.g. active filter label, count, or visible control state)? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 3 Acceptance 2]
- [ ] CHK012 Is "consistent order" when showing all projects defined (e.g. by date, featured first) so FR-009 acceptance scenario 3 is testable? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 3 Acceptance 3]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK013 Are credibility and scannability requirements (FR-001, FR-002) consistent with SC-001 (15-second scan, one concrete credibility signal)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, FR-002, SC-001]
- [ ] CHK014 Is the About page content required in FR-003 consistent with User Story 1 acceptance (headshot, background, social in one place)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, User Story 1]
- [ ] CHK015 Are theme descriptor requirements (FR-004) consistent across writing index and "Read Next" or related-content surfaces? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Can SC-001 ("first-time visitor can complete 15-second scan and identify who the site is for and one concrete credibility signal") be verified without implementation details? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK017 Are acceptance scenarios for User Story 1 (hero signal, section headings/summaries, About content, theme descriptors) independently testable? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 1]
- [ ] CHK018 Are acceptance scenarios for User Story 3 (filter/group, clear selection, all projects in consistent order) objectively verifiable? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 3]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK019 When there are zero writings in a theme, are requirements specified for how the theme appears on the writing index (e.g. hidden, or "no posts yet")? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK020 When the site has only one writing, is the theme descriptor requirement (FR-004) still applicable and testable? [Coverage, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements defined for the project filter zero-results state (empty state message, way to clear) in a functional requirement or only in Edge Cases? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009, Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK022 Is the edge case "project filter returns zero results" (empty state, way to clear) explicitly required in a testable FR? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK023 If the headshot or theme descriptor is missing or fails to load, are fallback or error display requirements specified? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- These items validate the quality of UX requirements as written; they do not verify that the UI implements them correctly.
- Each `/speckit.checklist` run creates a new file; this file focuses on UX (credibility, scannability, About, themes, projects) only.
